description: live_momentum re-entry-after-loss circuit breaker (N=1 on losses)

metrics.name: reentry_blocked rejects in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per loss-after-loss attempt; track >=10 in 14d for re-eval (Session 90 watch-list)

metrics.name: after-win re-entry direction
metrics.expectation: n=5 mixed; tighten if >=3/5 are losses

metrics.name: per-sport distribution of reentry_blocked rejects
metrics.expectation: if all cluster in one sport, evaluate per-sport MOMENTUM_REENTRY_LOSS_LIMIT override

description: vig_stack family cap enforced at executor entry boundary

metrics.name: new position_over_family_cap flags after this ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any fresh post-Session-92 flag means executor-level cap enforcement regressed or a non-vig_stack path is mislabeled

metrics.name: cap_exceeded_reject rejects in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 only when stale/manual/malformed vig_stack sizing exceeds family cap; normal scanner path should size down before this guard

description: vig_stack disabled on KXHIGHCHI and KXINX at executor entry boundary

metrics.name: family_disabled_reject events in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per scan that surfaces a KXHIGHCHI or KXINX vig_stack opportunity; 0 in 14d means the guard is dead code or scan stopped surfacing these families

metrics.name: new vig_stack positions opened on KXHIGHCHI or KXINX after ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any post-Session-93 entry on these families means the disable regressed

metrics.name: 30-day rolling P&L counterfactual: would-have-traded KXHIGHCHI/KXINX vig_stack
metrics.expectation: tracked manually via paper_trades.json filter; if positive sustained, reconsider re-enable; if negative or flat, disable validated

description: live_momentum disabled on atp + nba_game at live_watcher entry (breakeven-WR framework)

metrics.name: new live_momentum positions opened on atp or nba_game after ship
metrics.expectation: 0; any post-Session-97 entry on these sports means the disable regressed

metrics.name: sport_disabled rejects on atp + nba_game in decisions.jsonl
metrics.expectation: >=1 per scan that surfaces an atp or nba_game live_momentum opportunity; rising count means the entry guard is firing on live cohorts

metrics.name: shadow_trades.jsonl rows on atp + nba_game blocked_reason=sport_disabled
metrics.expectation: rising across 14d (Session 95 wiring auto-emits on each sport_disabled reject); if >=20 shadow rows hit settlement with aggregate WR > 55%, the disable was wrong and re-enable warranted; if WR < 40%, disable is data-confirmed

metrics.name: ufc live_momentum settled trade count (watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: re-run the breakeven analysis when N >= 15; currently n=11 with -23.9pp gap on lifetime but post-fix data sparse

description: snapshot_universe outer asyncio.wait_for timeout in _main_loop (600s hard cap)

metrics.name: SCAN CYCLE interval median over 24h (awake-hours window)
metrics.expectation: approx 30 min during awake hours under healthy Kalshi; pre-ship median was ~73 min over 48h (Phase 0.1 evidence). If post-ship median stays > 50 min during awake hours with snapshot_outer_timeout_count_24h == 0, a secondary cause exists (likely sync Kalshi calls in _main_loop steps 4-7b) and a Session 99 audit is warranted.

metrics.name: snapshot_outer_timeout_count_24h in bot_state.json
metrics.expectation: 0 under healthy Kalshi; >0 confirms the outer guard fired and abandoned a stuck scan. Counter resets daily alongside scans_today. If consistently >= 3/day for >= 3 days, Kalshi has degraded enough that per-request HTTP timeouts in agent/kalshi_client.py become the next ship.

metrics.name: longest SCAN CYCLE gap during awake hours
metrics.expectation: < 90 min after Session 98 (600s outer cap + 60s recovery sleep + 30 min IDLE wait = ~41 min max + slack). Pre-ship: 479 min observed May 10 03:08-11:07 UTC. If post-ship gaps still exceed 90 min, the outer timeout isn't firing as expected — inspect bot.log for the new ERROR line 'snapshot_universe outer-deadline 600s exceeded'.

description: live_momentum scalar fair-value proxy (estimated_win_prob) — Data Collection Backlog Priority 3 instrumentation, no sizing changes

metrics.name: live_momentum decisions with non-null estimated_win_prob in decisions.jsonl.extra
metrics.expectation: rising forward; non-null when wp_edge + leader_price both present (entry-decision sites at live_watcher.py:1422-1453). Stays null at scan-time sites where game_ctx=None — that's correct, not a regression.

metrics.name: live_momentum paper_trades records carrying estimated_win_prob
metrics.expectation: rising forward on accepted live_momentum trades. paper_trades.json filter: type==live_momentum AND estimated_win_prob present. Pre-Session-99 trades won't carry the field (forward-only).

metrics.name: tools/calibration_report.py renders live_momentum proxy section
metrics.expectation: yes; pre-trades shows 'ships forward-only; expect first calibration after ~14d' caveat; post-trades renders Brier + bias + bucket table

metrics.name: vig_stack records spillover of estimated_win_prob (regression-lock)
metrics.expectation: 0; any vig_stack paper_trades.json record carrying estimated_win_prob means a non-live_watcher writer started populating paper_estimated_win_prob in its opp dict. Investigate the writer; vig_stack should never produce this field.

metrics.name: Brier score on resolved live_momentum proxy calibration (watch-list trigger)
metrics.expectation: re-evaluate proxy model after 14d (target 2026-05-25). If Brier <= 0.20 across >= 30 resolved trades, the proxy is calibrated enough to consider confidence-weighted sizing in a future session. Brier > 0.25 means worse-than-random — investigate the v1 math identity (likely a game_context.win_probability quality issue, not a proxy bug).
